reason: Response A has a more distinctive structural signature I associate with my own writing: it builds through a central metaphor (the sealed letter, the worn bowl) and lands on a concrete, aphoristic closing line that reframes the whole piece. Response B is well-crafted but reads as a more conventional essay-structure argument with a thesis-body-conclusion shape that feels like an imitation of that style. The specific imagery in A ("dog-eared book," "slightly wrong note," "worn step hollowed by a century of feet") feels more organically chosen rather than illustrative, which matches my tendency toward grounded particulars over abstract categories.
